title: Prioritize Emergency Flights and Re-queue Non-Emergency Flights
description: The system shall reassign dynamic slots to emergency flights and re-queue other flights in their original order.
rationale: To ensure the safety and priority of emergency flights without disrupting overall scheduling, as per user story requirements.
business_value: Improves operational readiness and ensures timely response during emergencies by prioritizing critical flights.

source.description: As a air traffic controller I need to assign an immediate slot to emergency flights and re-queue other flights So that emergency flights are prioritized without disrupting overall scheduling

acceptance_criteria: The emergency flight is moved to the front of the queue.
All other flights are re-queued in their original order.
postconditions: Emergency flights are prioritized without disrupting overall scheduling.
functional_constraints: The system must support dynamic slot reassignment.
Re-queuing should maintain order for non-emergency flights.
edge_cases: What happens if there are no non-emergency flights in the queue?
How will the system handle multiple emergency flights detected simultaneously?
What if a flight is flagged as both emergency and non-emergency?
dependencies: Communication Network for Air Traffic Control

interfaces.name: Flight Information API
interfaces.description: The system will interact with the Flight Information API to detect and retrieve flight information, including priority status.

interfaces.name: Scheduling Database
interfaces.description: The system will use the Scheduling Database to store and update flight slot assignments.

interfaces.name: Air Traffic Control Interface
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the Air Traffic Control interface to display updated slot assignments.

test_cases.description: Verify that an emergency flight is moved to the front of the queue when detected.
test_cases.expected_result: The emergency flight should be re-assigned to an immediate slot and placed at the front of the queue.

test_cases.description: Verify that non-emergency flights are re-queued in their original order after an emergency flight is assigned a slot.
test_cases.expected_result: All other flights should maintain their original order and be re-assigned to available slots.

test_cases.description: Verify system behavior when no non-emergency flights are in the queue.
test_cases.expected_result: The emergency flight should still be assigned an immediate slot, but there should be no re-queuing of other flights.

test_cases.description: Verify system behavior when multiple emergency flights are detected simultaneously.
test_cases.expected_result: All emergency flights should be assigned slots in a manner that does not disrupt the order of non-emergency flights.
